reasoning: The most recent known value as of May 9, 2025 is 0.41. This represents a partial reversion from a brief local spike to 0.46 in late April. In the past year, the index has largely ranged between 0.32 and 0.40, making 0.41 locally elevated. Short-term momentum is decisively downward (falling 0.05 in two weeks), so for the earliest date (May 18), continued mean reversion and flat prints (which resolve as NO) make a decrease highly probable (P(>0.41) = 0.37). For June 10, local mean-reversion forces remain strong, but uncertainty grows. Over medium-to-longer horizons (August and November), different dynamics take over. Currently, investment-grade spreads are near historical lows (19th percentile over multiple years). Because credit spreads are bounded near zero but have significant right skew during economic shocks, a longer time horizon increases the probability of returning to long-run historical averages (which are higher than 0.41). This pulls the 3-month forecast closer to 50/50, and the 6-month forecast (November) crosses over to slightly greater than 0.50, correctly reflecting the asymmetrical upside risk inherent in historically tight credit spreads. These estimates strictly adhere to prioritizing increasing uncertainty over time.
